children.name: Polioxolmis
children.description: Polioxolmis — a genus of tyrant flycatchers in the family Tyrannidae.

children.name: Polioxolmis rufipennis
children.description: The rufous-webbed bush tyrant is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, and Peru.

children.name: Pseudocolopteryx
children.description: Pseudocolopteryx — a genus of tyrant flycatchers in the family Tyrannidae.

children.name: Pseudocolopteryx acutipennis
children.description: The subtropical doradito is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, and Peru.

children.name: Pseudocolopteryx citreola
children.description: The ticking doradito is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, and possibly Paraguay.

children.name: Pseudocolopteryx dinelliana
children.description: Dinelli's doradito is a Near Threatened species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Paraguay, and possibly Brazil.

children.name: Pseudocolopteryx flaviventris
children.description: The warbling doradito is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Paraguay, Uruguay and Brazil.

children.name: Pseudocolopteryx sclateri
children.description: The crested doradito is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Guyana, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Venezuela, possibly in French Guiana, and as a vagrant to Trinidad.

children.name: Tachuris
children.description: Tachuris — a genus of tyrant flycatchers in the family Tyrannidae.

children.name: Tachuris rubrigastra
children.description: The many-colored rush tyrant or many-coloured rush tyrant is a small passerine bird of South America belonging to the tyrant flycatcher family Tyrannidae. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Paraguay, Peru, and Uruguay.

children.name: Conopias
children.description: Conopias — a genus of tyrant flycatchers in the family Tyrannidae.

children.name: Conopias albovittatus
children.description: The white-ringed flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Honduras, Nicaragua, and Panama.

children.name: Conopias cinchoneti
children.description: The lemon-browed flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Venezuela, and possibly Bolivia.

children.name: Conopias trivirgatus
children.description: The three-striped flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Ecuador, Paraguay, Peru, and Venezuela.

children.description: The southern bristle tyrant is a Least Concern species of passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, and Paraguay.

children.name: Phylloscartes gualaquizae
children.description: The Ecuadorian tyrannulet is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Colombia, Ecuador and Peru.

children.description: The strange-tailed tyrant is a Vulnerable species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina and Paraguay and as a vagrant in Brazil and Uruguay.

children.name: Alectrurus tricolor
children.description: The cock-tailed tyrant is a Vulnerable species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, and Paraguay.

children.name: Hirundinea ferruginea
children.description: The cliff flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is known in every mainland South American country except Chile and may occur there as well.

children.description: The ochre-faced tody-flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, Peru, and Uruguay.

children.name: Aphanotriccus audax
children.description: The black-billed flycatcher is a Near Threatened species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Colombia and Panama.

children.name: Aphanotriccus capitalis
children.description: The tawny-chested flycatcher is a small Vulnerable species of passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Costa Rica and previously was also found in Nicaragua.

children.name: Zimmerius vilissimus
children.description: The Guatemalan tyrannulet, or paltry tyrannulet, is a very small passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Belize, El Salvador, Guatemala, and Mexico.

children.name: Zimmerius villarejoi
children.description: The Mishana tyrannulet is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is endemic to Peru.

children.name: Zimmerius viridiflavus
